# Oracle Health — Bi-Weekly Domain Intelligence Synthesis
**Date**: March 23, 2026
**Domains Updated**: Payer | Life Sciences | Health Systems | Interoperability | RCM | ERP

## 1. MACRO SIGNAL OF THE CYCLE

**The CMS Regulatory Wave is Hitting All Three Core Domains Simultaneously — and Oracle is the Only Vendor Positioned Across All Three.**

The CMS-0057-F rule (Prior Authorization FHIR mandates) is not a single-domain event. It is triggering urgent buying behavior across Payer, Interoperability, and RCM at the same time:

- **Payer**: Public prior authorization performance metrics are due March 31, 2026 (8 days). Mid-market Medicare Advantage plans that stumbled on FHIR implementation are in remediation mode NOW.
- **Interoperability**: The FHIR PA API hard mandate goes live January 1, 2027. Oracle is a designated TEFCA QHIN since November 2025 — the only EHR vendor with a live, production-grade FHIR exchange network already running.
- **RCM**: CMS prior auth rules require 72-hour urgent and 7-day standard decision timelines. AI denial prevention tools that act upstream at clinical documentation are the only viable response.

**What this means for Oracle's overall strategy**: Oracle has a rare and time-bounded opportunity to market a single, unified "Oracle Healthcare AI Compliance Platform" story that resolves all three regulatory pain points simultaneously. No competitor — not Epic, not Waystar, not Cotiviti — has the EHR-native + payer + FHIR + RCM combined stack. Oracle does. This is the quarter to frame it as a platform narrative rather than selling modules in isolation.

---

## 2. CROSS-DOMAIN OPPORTUNITIES

### Opportunity A: Payer + Interoperability — "FHIR Compliance as a Platform"
**The Play**: Bundle CMS-0057-F remediation (Payer AI Prior Auth module) + QHIN FHIR exchange activation into a single "PA Compliance in 90 Days" offer. Target mid-market Medicare Advantage plans (500K–2M members) that missed the March 31 public metrics baseline. Oracle's FHIR-native architecture and QHIN designation make this the only credible fast-path to compliance. Competitors (Cotiviti, Edifecs) solve the data problem but not the workflow problem. Oracle solves both.
**Estimated pipeline**: 5–8 enterprise conversations in Q2 2026. Revenue model: platform subscription + implementation services.

### Opportunity B: Life Sciences + Health Systems — "Oracle RWE Studio on the Learning Health Network"
**The Play**: Oracle's 143-health-system Learning Health Network (129M+ de-identified records) is the infrastructure layer. The FDA's December 2025 removal of individual patient data requirements for RWE submissions makes this a direct regulatory submission asset. Package as "Oracle RWE Studio" — synthetic control arm generation + cohort identification + de-identification documentation — and sell to pharma CROs as a per-study engagement ($500K–$2M). The health systems provide the data, Oracle monetizes the access, pharma sponsors fund the study.
**Why now**: No other vendor has EHR-native RWE data at this scale with FDA-compliant de-identification. IQVIA launched IQVIA.ai on March 16 and will pivot to this space within 90 days.

### Opportunity C: RCM + ERP — "The Full-Stack Oracle Healthcare Operating System"
**The Play**: Oracle is the only vendor that sells EHR + RCM + ERP + HCM + Supply Chain as a single integrated platform. Health system margins are at a 12-month low (-0.6% January 2026) with supply costs up 12.3% YOY. The CFO and COO are the new buyers — and they want a cost story, not a clinical story. The SAP IS-H discontinuation and Infor Lawson 2030 EOL are forcing hundreds of large health systems into ERP evaluations RIGHT NOW. Oracle can lead with "one vendor, one contract, one data model" and close EHR + ERP deals simultaneously that no other vendor can match.

---

## 3. TOP 3 INTELLIGENCE ALERTS

### 🔴 Alert 1 — PAYER + INTEROPERABILITY — ACT NOW
**Signal**: CMS-0057-F public prior authorization performance metrics are due March 31, 2026 — 8 days from now. Plans that failed to meet FHIR implementation requirements will have their deficiencies publicly visible. This is the single best near-term sales trigger Oracle has this quarter.
**Action needed**: Build and release the "CMS-0057-F PA Compliance Scorecard" sales asset by March 31. Frame it as: "How did your plan score? Oracle can fix it in 90 days." Distribute to compliance officers and CIOs at mid-market MA plans. Mike: brief field teams on this NOW.

### 🔴 Alert 2 — LIFE SCIENCES — RESPOND THIS WEEK
**Signal**: IQVIA launched IQVIA.ai on March 16, 2026 (7 days ago) powered by NVIDIA with 150+ AI agents deployed and 19 of the top 20 pharma companies already running IQVIA agents. IQVIA is directly entering Oracle's Life Sciences AI Data Platform territory with massive existing relationships.
**Action needed**: Oracle Life Sciences team needs a counter-narrative this week. The answer is the FDA RWE story (Dec 2025 barrier removal + 129M records). IQVIA cannot replicate EHR-native RWE data at Oracle's scale. Mike: this needs to get to the Life Sciences product GM before the end of the month.

### 🟡 Alert 3 — HEALTH SYSTEMS — STRATEGIC THREAT
**Signal**: Microsoft announced an Epic-on-Azure partnership in March 2026, repositioning Azure as the preferred cloud infrastructure for Epic customers. This directly neutralizes Oracle's OCI cloud differentiation narrative in health system deals — especially in accounts already running Epic.
**Action needed**: Oracle field teams need updated messaging that reframes the cloud story from "OCI vs. Azure" to "Oracle Health as a complete governed healthcare operating system — EHR + ERP + AI on a single sovereign cloud." The infrastructure story alone no longer wins.

## 4. COMPETITIVE THREAT MAP

| Competitor | Active Domains | Overall Strategy | Threat Level |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Epic Systems** | Health Systems, Interoperability, RCM | Expanding market share (largest net gain in history 2024), forcing TEFCA adoption industry-wide, Azure cloud partnership for infrastructure play | 🔴 CRITICAL |
| **IQVIA** | Life Sciences | IQVIA.ai launch (March 16) with NVIDIA, 150+ agents, 19/20 top pharma — racing to own AI narrative before Oracle establishes RWE position | 🔴 HIGH |
| **Waystar** | RCM | AI-first "autonomous revenue cycle" narrative, $1.1B FY2025 (+17% YoY), #1 Black Book AI RCM, actively pitching Oracle installed base | 🟠 HIGH |
| **Cotiviti + Edifecs** | Payer, Interoperability | 2026 Best in KLAS for CMS Payer Interoperability (91.9 score), own the compliance narrative, founding Da Vinci member | 🟠 HIGH |
| **Workday** | ERP | 9th consecutive KLAS Best in KLAS for ERP, strong CFO/CHRO relationships, racing to capture SAP IS-H and Infor Lawson displacement | 🟠 HIGH |
| **Microsoft (Azure)** | Health Systems, Interoperability | Epic-on-Azure partnership absorbs cloud differentiation narrative; Azure API for FHIR retires Sept 30, 2026 creating migration chaos | 🟡 MEDIUM |

**Overall thesis**: Epic is executing a multi-domain offensive simultaneously. Microsoft is flanking via infrastructure. IQVIA is a new entrant with massive pharma relationships. Oracle's response must be platform-level, not product-level.

---

## 5. THE ONE MOVE FOR MIKE THIS BI-WEEK

**Brief Matt Cohlmia and the Oracle Health field leadership with a single unified regulatory narrative — this week, before March 31.**

Here's the setup: Three Oracle domains (Payer, Interoperability, RCM) are converging on the same CMS regulatory forcing function — CMS-0057-F. Oracle is uniquely positioned across all three. The public performance metrics dropping March 31 will create visible pain for dozens of health plans simultaneously. This is the most concentrated near-term sales catalyst in all six domain updates.

**The pitch**: "Oracle Health is the only vendor that can resolve your CMS prior authorization compliance, FHIR exchange, and claims denial burden with one platform, one implementation, and one data model. In 90 days, your performance metrics improve. In 12 months, you've replaced three vendors with one."

**Mike's specific action**: Prepare a one-page "CMS-0057-F Cross-Domain Response" brief for Matt Cohlmia by March 28. Frame it as Oracle's strategic opportunity, not a product pitch. Three domains, one regulatory wave, one Oracle answer. Suggest a coordinated field enablement push the week of March 31 when the public metrics attract board-level attention.
